_meta.authored_by: project's resident 日本語教師 persona
_meta.note: Production-side metadata for IMP-094 future native-recording session. Not learner-facing — kept out of listening.json so JA-13 (N5-scope-kanji invariant) does not reject the technical Japanese vocabulary used in directions.

prosody_hints.note: Narrator scene-set + question. Read flatter than dialogue lines. Mild prosodic peak on どこ in the closing question.
Casual-friendly invitation. The 〜ませんか tag is rising, but only mildly — not gushing.
Receptive いいですね then a clean 何時に — keep it neutral, no excitement.
三時に slows slightly to give the listener a chance to register the time. NOT a wh-word, no peak.
This is the resolution turn. えきの前は人がおおい sets up the rejection; カフェの前で会いましょう lands the answer. Both halves equally clear, NO emphasis on カフェの前 — that would telegraph it.
はい、わかりました — closing acknowledgement. Brisk, not slow.

do_not: Do not put extra emphasis on カフェの前 — that is the correct answer; emphasis is the listener's tell. Both location options must sound equally salient.
Do not pause inside えきの前は人がおおいですね — the comma is a written aid, not a vocalised pause longer than ~150ms.

n5.listen.004.pacing: 180-220 morae/min; service-counter exchange so slightly brisker turn-taking (~200ms).

prosody_hints.note: Narrator. Mild peak on 何 in the closing question.
いらっしゃいませ — standard service-counter greeting. Bright but professional, NOT overly cheerful (no fake-anime lilt).
すみません as turn-opener; コーヒーをください is the nominal request. Both halves clean.
あついコーヒーですか — confirmation question. Rising tag, polite but slightly probing.
いいえ rejects あつい; つめたいコーヒーで is the actual answer. NO extra emphasis on つめたい — both temperatures must sound equally weighted.
Final confirmation. Calm.

do_not: Do not over-emphasise つめたい — it is the correct answer; emphasis would telegraph it.
Do not anglicise コーヒー. It is 4 morae in Japanese (こ・ー・ひ・ー), not 2 syllables in English.
Do not skip the long ー on コーヒー — that is the most common synthetic-engine error this brief is correcting.

n5.listen.005.pacing: 180-210 morae/min; ~250ms turn-pause. The student's lines should sound contrite without being theatrical.

prosody_hints.note: Narrator. Mild peak on どうして in the closing question.
先生 question, mildly stern but not angry. どうして carries the wh-word peak.
Student's すみません apologetic, then あさ、でんしゃがおくれました — flat, factual. NO emotional colour.
先生 receives the explanation neutrally. そうですか is acknowledgement, not approval.
はい、もうだいじょうぶです — student's reassurance. Calm.

do_not: Do not under-emphasise でんしゃがおくれました — it is the correct reason; the listener should hear it just as clearly as the distractors. But also do NOT over-emphasise it.
Do not soften 先生 into a sympathetic tone. The pedagogical point is that the student must report the reason; the teacher's tone is neutral-authoritative throughout.

n5.listen.008.pacing: 180-210 morae/min; conversational so turn-pauses can shrink to ~200ms.

prosody_hints.note: Narrator. Mild peak on 何 in the closing question.
女: probing question with two distinct candidates (本, とけい). Both candidates equally weighted.
男: 本もいいですが is concessive — gentle rising-falling on いいですが to mark "but". The もっとほしいものがあります sets up the reveal.
女: clarifying question. とけい still in candidate weight.
男: いいえ rejects とけい. 新しいカメラがほしいです lands the answer. NO extra emphasis on カメラ — equal-weight rule.
そうですか — closing acknowledgement. Calm.

do_not: Do not over-emphasise カメラ. The distractors 本 and とけい must sound equally salient.
Do not anglicise カメラ — Japanese atamadaka, drop on め.

n5.listen.009.pacing: ~180 morae/min — slow side of the band because each utterance is short.

prosody_hints.note: Narrator scene-set. Flat, slightly slower. The parenthetical aside should NOT sound like dialogue — it is stage direction.
All three candidate utterances should be read by the SAME voice with EQUAL prosodic weight. The listener decides on words, not delivery.

pronunciation_callouts.reading: すみません (heiban; do not over-emphasise the apologetic register)

do_not: Do not give the correct candidate (すみません、いま何時ですか) any extra polish. The wrong candidates must be read just as fluently.
Do not vary speaker between candidates — that would add a non-content cue.
Pre-roll the narrator scene-set with ~500ms of silence; this gives the listener the conventional "now I should think" moment.

prosody_hints.note: Narrator scene-set: 友だちのいえへ入ります。 Flat, slightly slower. NOT a question.
All four candidate utterances read by the same voice, equal prosodic weight. しつれいします (correct) is the most formal-sounding by content; do not let prosody amplify that. Read it as flatly as いただきます.

do_not: Do not make しつれいします sound more "polite" than the others — content already encodes that. The listener has to recognise the social context, not detect emphasis.
Do not laugh-track or warm-tone any of the candidates — Mondai 3 is decoy-heavy and tone-clean.

n5.listen.041.pacing: ~200 morae/min — these items are short; do not rush, but also do not pad.

prosody_hints.note: Stimulus: おさきにしつれいします — leaving the office. Slight politeness register but not overly formal. Crisp; ~2-3 seconds delivered.
Three candidates read by the SAME male voice, equal weight. おつかれさまでした (correct) is the formulaic workplace pair; do not let prosody amplify it.

do_not: Do not warm おつかれさまでした relative to はじめまして — equal weight rule.
Pre-roll on the stimulus is ~300ms (not the 500ms of mondai 3); these items are tight.
Inter-candidate pause: ~400-500ms — long enough that the listener registers the pivot from one candidate to the next.

prosody_hints.note: Stimulus: おはようございます — morning greeting. Bright but not overly cheerful. The brightness comes from the time-of-day register, not from the actor.

Three candidate responses by the SAME female voice. Critical: おはようございます (correct response) must NOT sound brighter than the wrong おやすみなさい — both are time-of-day greetings; the listener has to map the time, not detect tone.

do_not: Do not give おはようございます candidate any extra brightness vs おやすみなさい. Same time-of-day-greeting register means same prosodic profile.
さようなら is the universal farewell — keep it neutral, no sad lilt.
